evidence.scientific_clarity: All three numbered equations (ELBO, decomposition, retrieval-augmented variant) are present with surrounding prose that correctly names Jensen's inequality, KL divergences, and retrieved exemplars R(x); the derivation chain is legible and scientifically accurate.
evidence.typography: pdflatex produces a real Type-1 PDF with LM (Latin Modern) fonts — LMRoman, LMMathItalic, LMMathSymbols, MSBM — not DejaVu Sans; text is fully selectable; no fonttype=42 matplotlib setting needed since this is a native LaTeX document.
evidence.palette: This is a black-and-white mathematical typesetting document — no colour palette is used at all; neither colorblind issues nor directional-colour discipline apply, but the absence of any visual colour encoding is a mild miss for a 'derivation block' that could highlight equation labels or key terms.
evidence.layout_density: Single-page layout with a title, three numbered equations interspersed with explanatory prose, and equations numbered (1)–(3) at the right margin; whitespace is appropriate for a math document with no excessive gaps or overlapping labels.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption correctly cross-references \eqref{eq:elbo}, \eqref{eq:decomp}, and \eqref{eq:retro}; all three equations mentioned in the caption match what appears in the figure; no legend needed for a derivation block; Reviewer 2 could reasonably accept this as-is.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is produced by pdfTeX-1.40.29 and contains embedded Type-1 PostScript font programs (LMRoman, LMMathItalic, MSBM10, etc.) with CMap streams; text is fully selectable and infinitely scalable — true vector output.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (158 KB, real vector) and figure.png (200 dpi raster) are present; caption.tex is present and non-trivial; gen_figure.py is present; however equations.tex (the intermediate source) was not preserved in the output directory as required by the brief.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains all three equations by name and role (ELBO, KL decomposition, retrieval-augmented ELBO conditioned on R(x) of k exemplars), cites the derivation step (Jensen's inequality), and specifically identifies RetroDiff's contribution; includes the concrete symbol k for the retrieved-exemplar set size.

overall_strengths: Genuine pdflatex-compiled derivation block with proper LaTeX math environments, equation numbering, \eqref cross-references, and publication-quality Latin Modern fonts — exactly what the brief requested.

Caption is detailed and scientifically precise, naming each equation, its mathematical role, and the novel contribution (retrieval-augmented ELBO), enabling a reader unfamiliar with the paper to understand the figure without reading the body text.
overall_weaknesses: equations.tex source file was not preserved in the working directory alongside figure.pdf/figure.png and caption.tex as the brief explicitly required, making the output set incomplete.
No colour or visual differentiation is used; while acceptable for a derivation block, key labels or the novel equation (3) could be highlighted to draw the reader's eye to the main contribution.
